title: Dehydrated Sweet Venezuelan Plantain Empanadas
description: A sour and snappy nostalgic Venezuelan dish featuring granulated sugar, these empanadas are paleo-friendly and perfect for a sweet treat. Made with dehydrated plantains and a touch of sugar, they are a delicious and healthy snack.
instructions: Preheat the dehydrator to 135 degrees Fahrenheit.
Peel the plantains and slice them into 1/4 inch thick rounds.
In a large bowl, mix together the sugar, salt, and coconut oil.
Add the sliced plantains to the bowl and toss to coat with the sugar mixture.
Dehydrate the plantains for 6 hours or until they are dry and slightly flexible.
In a separate bowl, mix together the almond flour and coconut flour.
Add the dehydrated plantains to the bowl and mix until they are well coated with the flour mixture.
Use the egg to make an egg wash and brush the edges of the empanada dough.
Spoon some of the plantain mixture onto the center of the empanada dough and fold the dough in half to form a triangle.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the egg wash and dehydrate for an additional 30 minutes.
